steps.title: Kindergarten
steps.finding: Kindergarten intake is small but consistently on or above target.
steps.action: Sustain early-registration drives to protect the feeder pipeline.

steps.title: Elementary
steps.finding: A -7 dip in 2025-2026, then stabilizing near the target (-1) in 2026-2027.
steps.action: Track transfer-out reasons and strengthen learner retention in the elementary cohort.

steps.title: Junior High
steps.finding: Actual enrolment consistently exceeded target in both years targets were set (+4, then +3).
steps.action: Set slightly more ambitious targets and plan section/teacher load for continued growth.

steps.title: Senior High
steps.finding: SHS enrolment declined from 27 to 19 due to transfers to schools offering Cookery / Automotive.
steps.action: Build on the 6 new computer units to make GAS / ICT-CSS strands more competitive.

steps.title: Cohort Tracking
steps.finding: Grade 3 and Grade 6 grew, while Kindergarten, Grade 2, Grade 5, and JHS Grade 7 / Grade 10 contracted.
steps.action: Strengthen Grade 6 to 7 transition and Grade 10 to 11 bridging into Senior High School.

domains.name: Instructional Equipment
bullets: Smart TVs in all classrooms; printers for all teachers, laptops for some.
Science & Mathematics equipment available and actively used.
ICT tools remain uneven across teachers, affecting consistency.

domains.name: ICT Environment
bullets: Rising SHS ICT Programming enrolment raises equipment demand.
Existing resources are limited relative to curriculum needs.
Hands-on ICT competencies are the most affected.

domains.name: Assessment
bullets: Regional Unified Quarterly Exams standardise assessment.
Pacing does not always align with exam coverage.
HOTS / NAT / PISA capacity-building and ICT for data analysis needed.

domains.name: Teachers
bullets: Small, fluctuating classes mean multi-level loads and flexible deployment.
Teachers handle multiple areas and grade levels, esp. JHS and SHS.
SHS ICT Programming raises specialisation demands.

domains.name: Instructional Materials
bullets: Limited provision; many resources outdated (pandemic-era).
MATATAG rollout delayed and staggered — started Grades 1 & 7.
Uneven access to updated materials across grade levels.

domains.name: Facilities
bullets: 14 classrooms, incl. temporary Marcos-type structures for SHS.
Missing: computer lab, clinic, DRRM room, Teen Center.
Home Economics room dilapidated, now used as storage.

domains.name: School Leadership
bullets: Operating amid program expansion and SHS validation.
Managing enrolment swings, compliance and limited resources.
Needs stronger forecasting, optimisation and supervision.

domains.name: SDO Technical Assistance
bullets: Training, staffing, ICT, assessment, supervision & governance support.
Strong for compliance and initial implementation needs.
Needs sustained coaching and on-site technical assistance.

domains.name: Community & Partnerships
bullets: Strong PTA, Barangay LGU and School Governing Council support.
Backed early SHS / TVL support and work immersion.
Engagement still developing in depth and sustainability.
